title: Create appointment booking portal backend
body:
Book appointment in a way that persists (store bookings in cosmosdb, or existing calendar integration)

We do not have to store every opening, just only store any appointments made. For the view that the lead sees for open slots, we can query appointments, see what is already booked on a specified day .

Example: Lead wants to book appointment tomorrow. System queries 2026-03-25 and gets one result; one appointment with timeslot 10 booked. The view populates an array of open slots the client can choose, meaning every other hour between 9-5pm besides 10 am, meaning 9 am and 11-5pm -> 9 and 11 to 17

Alternatively, the client does not get to manually pick a slot. They reply to the email, and the system books it using GPT 4.1 mini interpreting their response. This one has more room for error though so I don't know if we want to do that.
Ensure booking sync between clients for open bookings.

Notify lead & dealership about the booking, like by email .ics files

Appointment editing/cancellation is NOT in scope; we do not have to implement this

title: Define appointment document structure (NoSQL)
body:
- Store booking times in a NoSQL container? `appointments`
- **9-5 pm** Mon-Fri, 1 hr pre-set slots (ex. 9 am, 10 am, 11 am, etc)
appointments container
Partition key: /dealerID
{
  "dealerId": "dealer_7f3b2a9e1c",
  "vehicleId": "vehicle_4d9e1a8b2f",
  "leadId": "lead_9c8d7e6f5a",
  "conversationId": "conv_1a2b3c4d5e",
  "appointmentDate": "2026-03-24", # ISO 8601 yyyy-mm-dd
  "timeslot": "14", # 2 pm
  "status": 0 # 0 = not booked, 1 = booked
}

title: Create appointment booking portal frontend (React)
body:
Populate with open time slots that syncs on refresh on opening the view. 

The only frontend view that is required is this view where lead can see available slots on a given day. We need this view to enforce appointment sync when they open the link. 

Example: Lead wants to book appointment tomorrow. System queries 2026-03-25 and gets one result; one appointment with timeslot 10 booked. The view populates an array of open slots the client can choose, meaning every other hour between 9-5pm besides 10 am, meaning 9 am and 11-5pm -> 9 and 11 to 17

Alternatively, I don't know if we can embed anything into email that refreshes... if we can, that is another option.

title: Prompt finetuning
body:
Improve behavior to make it feel more natural.

- RN it repeats the vehicle stats almost in full each reply; skip this after the first email unless requested
- It sounds very "AI." Could be more naturalistic.
- Too friendly maybe? Should be more brutally honest, ie if a student wants a Porsche, it should say that it is unrealistic but they are happy to find alternatives for them.

- Limitations: false positives (e.g., flagging an employee working while on vacation), AI is not perfect

body:
Research and create slides

Member 3: Tools & Industry Examples

- Microsoft Defender for Cloud: monitors Azure resources and flags suspicious activity using built-in AI
- AWS GuardDuty: continuously watches cloud activity logs for signs of malicious behavior
- Anything else third market

title: Member 1: Why; What's the Problem?

milestone.title: AI-Driven Cloud Security (Threat Detection & Identity Risk) Presentation

body:
Research and create slides

Member 1: Why; What's the Problem?

- Cloud environments generate millions of logs per second; manual monitoring is not feasible at this scale
- The shift from static defense (firewalls, rules) to active intelligence (AI-driven detection)
- Common ways attackers get in: stolen passwords, impersonating legitimate users, exploiting over-privileged accounts
- Real-world example: Capital One AWS breach (2019)

title: Refactor email-processing-service to send via Microsoft Graph API instead of ACS
body:
Replace Azure Communication Services (ACS) with Microsoft Graph API for outbound email sending in the Durable Function. This is a blocking change, so all email processing work should be on hold until this is merged from its feature branch.

- Replace EmailClient and ACS connection string with an authenticated Graph API client using client creds
- Update send_email activity to POST to /v1.0/me/sendMail or /v1.0/users/{id}/sendMail instead of using the ACS poller pattern
- Temporarily remove `store_message` function to ensure the refactoring process works
- Update environment variables and remove ACS_CONNECTION_STRING and any other dependencies accordingly
- Validate that conversationId (our email threading id) is retrievable from Graph after send so it can be stored on the conversation record

title: Prompt sentiment & intent analysis (gpt-4.1 mini)

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
This is the analysis step that runs prior to reply generation: when a new inbound email is received, the email-processing-service runs a dedicated sentiment and intent analysis prompt against GPT-4.1 mini before any reply is constructed. The output is stored in `messages` and passed on to the reply generation activity function by the orchestrator.

- Run a prompt thru GPT-4.1 mini scoped only to classification; no reply generation in this step. Prompt must instruct the model to return ONLY a valid JSON object with no prose or explanation. Review [prompt & intent taxonomy planning](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wHahfUJDdmyAKJxrRkTXH2aZyR6RQWlMjBsrmCD3_W8/edit?tab=t.aunte3wpykqr)
- Validate that the response is valid JSON and all values are in defined taxonomy before storing. If parsing or validation fails, treat as intentConfidence: 0 and escalate to human.
- Store the analysis result to the messages record with the fields as defined by the new `messages` schema here: [NoSQL schema](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wHahfUJDdmyAKJxrRkTXH2aZyR6RQWlMjBsrmCD3_W8/edit?tab=t.omo2o45yn4gz)
- Evaluate escalation rules against the result before passing to reply generation activity: [prompt & intent taxonomy planning](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wHahfUJDdmyAKJxrRkTXH2aZyR6RQWlMjBsrmCD3_W8/edit?tab=t.aunte3wpykqr)
- Trend-based escalation maybe where we query the last 3 messages on the conversation before finalizing escalation decision.

title: PII reinjection

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
Restore PII to the raw LLM output, dispatch the outbound email, and persist the final state. Keeping this feature separate from #63 makes generation and delivery independently testable locally.

- Use the PII placeholder mapping established to reinsert original values into the LLM output; validate no placeholders before sending
- call_activity on send_email and store_message as usual, with changes as required to adapt the template to the LLM output

title: Implement PII sanitization layer

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
PII must be stripped before any content is sent to the LLM and reliably reinserted afterward. This should be a distinct activity function within the Durable Function.

**mostly completed in `feature/pii` in email-processing-service**
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/language-service/personally-identifiable-information/how-to/redact-text-pii

- Research the Foundry tool for PII detection and redaction or libraries like presidio
- Replace PII w/ placeholder tokens to be reinserted later in the Durable Function chain

title: Implement inbound/response email webhook
body:
If we have time, we should prepare our system to receive replies from the lead. It'll be good to establish that endpoint for future sprints.

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/communication-services/how-tos/call-automation/secure-webhook-endpoint?pivots=programming-language-python

- ACS -> webhook -> Function (can do nothing for now)
- Should be able to parse sender & body, and tie it to the conversation chain via email 
- Insert into `messages` array or table

title: Implement ACS email sender function (Python)
body:
Sends outbound emails. Function is triggered by messages popped out of queue in ServiceBus `OutboundEmail`. Therefore, email sending is decoupled from other processes.

- Service Bus/Queue triggered function
- ACS email client integration
- Send test email
- Retry logic & error handling
